title: Kashi system
content:

|region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="Sector">{{CSWECite|II|201|Kashi Mer}} places the Kashi [[Star system/Legends|system]] in the [[Phelleem sector/Legends|Phelleem sector]], which {{SW|url=news/essential-atlas-outer-rim-sectors|text=The Essential Atlas Extra: Outer Rim Sectors|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} establishes as part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]—specifically what ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' identifies as [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the region.</ref>

{{Quote|The Kashi Guardians of Breath still persist, despite the ancient destruction of their homeworld by a supernova.|[[Restelly Quist]], ''[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force]]''|<ref name="JP">[[The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force (real-life book)|''The Jedi Path: A Manual for Students of the Force'']]</ref>}}
The '''Kashi system''' was a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Phelleem sector/Legends|Phelleem sector]].<ref name="Sector" />

{{System-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Kashi system was first mentioned in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure "[[Relic (article)|Relic]]," which was authored by [[George R. Strayton]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[May]] [[1995]] [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 6|sixth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' magazine.<ref name="Journal6">{{JournalCite|6|Relic (article)}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the system in grid square S-7.<ref name="TEA" />
